title: Herbal Teas for Gum Pain: Top 10 Recommendations
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum pain are natural remedies that help alleviate discomfort, inflammation, and infection associated with gum disease. These teas contain bioactive compounds that reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and promote healing. The best herbal teas for gum pain include Zingiber officinale, Echinacea purpurea, Salvia officinalis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Camellia sinensis, Achillea millefolium, Mentha x piperita, Melissa officinalis, Eucalyptus globulus, and Cinnamomum verum. If left untreated, gum pain can lead to chronic dental issues, social isolation, and even cardiovascular problems, significantly impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a good remedy for gum pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink this tea, the chemicals in it help reduce swelling in the gums, which is often the cause of pain. This plant is also rich in antioxidants, which fight off free radicals that can cause infection and irritation in the gums. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea has antimicrobial properties that help kill bacteria that can cause gum disease and lead to pain. By drinking this tea, you can help soothe and calm your gums, reducing the pain and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties also help to ease inflammation in the mouth, making it easier to eat and speak without pain.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for gum pain because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by blocking the production of chemicals that cause inflammation.
Quercetin is good for gum pain because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.
Cichoric acid is good for gum pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it a natural remedy for soothing and healing gum problems.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for gum pain
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal tea for gum pain relief, start by gathering dried calendula flowers. You can find dried calendula flowers at health food stores or online. Begin by boiling one cup of water in a pot and then add one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the water has cooled slightly. Next, strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the calendula flowers. Add a tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea, if desired, and then let it cool to a comfortable temperature before drinking. To use the tea for gum pain relief, swish the cooled tea around in your mouth for a few minutes to help reduce inflammation and ease pain, then spit it out. Repeat this process as needed, ideally several times a day, to help alleviate gum pain and promote healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis tea is a good choice for gum pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The tea contains compounds like tannins and flavonoids that can fight off bacteria and reduce inflammation. Gum pain is often caused by infection or irritation, and Salvia officinalis tea can help calm the affected area. It can also help soothe mouth ulcers and gum sores, making it easier to eat and speak. Additionally, the tea has antibacterial properties that can prevent the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which can help prevent gum pain in the future.

Polyphenols are good for gum pain because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and molecules.

remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal teas for gum pain
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal tea for gum pain relief, start by gathering dried Calendula flowers or purchasing them from a health food store. Begin by boiling one cup of water in a pot, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Next, add a teaspoon of the dried Calendula flowers to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the tea will be. After the steeping time, strain the tea into a cup using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth. Allow the tea to cool down slightly, then use it as a mouthwash by swishing the liquid around your mouth for 30 seconds to a minute before spitting it out. Alternatively, you can also drink the tea as a warm beverage to help ease gum pain and inflammation. It is recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, has been used for centuries to help with gum pain. The main reason it works is because of its anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink ginger tea, the active compounds in the ginger, like gingerol and shogaol, are absorbed into your body. These compounds then travel to your mouth and gum area, where they reduce swelling and ease pain. This is especially helpful for people with gum disease or inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce infection and kill bacteria that can cause pain and discomfort. Additionally, ginger has natural soothing properties that can calm the gums and mouth, making it easier to eat and drink. When you combine these properties with the natural antibacterial and antiseptic properties of ginger, it's no wonder that ginger tea has been used for so long to help with gum pain.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for gum pain because it reduces inflammation and fights off infection in the gums, helping to ease pain and discomfort.

Shogaols help reduce gum pain by blocking pain receptors and reducing inflammation in the gums.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal tea for gum pain, you will need dried Calendula flowers and a tea infuser or a small pot. Start by boiling water in a pot, then add one teaspoon of dried Calendula flowers for every cup of water you are using. Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes, allowing the flowers to release their healing properties into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using the infuser or a small strainer, and discard the flowers. You can add honey to sweeten the tea if desired, but make sure to use a honey that is safe for gum health. To use the tea for gum pain, swish it around in your mouth like you would mouthwash, then spit it out. Repeat this process several times a day as needed to help soothe and reduce gum pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a popular remedy for gum pain because it contains a compound called eucalyptol. Eucalyptol has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. This can be especially helpful for people with gum infections or inflammation caused by gum disease. The eucalyptol in Eucalyptus globulus tea also has a natural antibacterial effect, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria in the gums that can cause pain and infection. By drinking Eucalyptus globulus tea, the eucalyptol is absorbed into the bloodstream and can reach the affected area in the mouth, providing quick relief from gum pain. Additionally, Eucalyptus globulus tea has been shown to have a numbing effect, which can help numb the pain and discomfort in the gums, making it a soothing and effective treatment for gum pain.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal tea that helps with gum pain, first you need to dry the calendula flowers properly. You can do this by tying the flowers in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry, and dark place. Let them dry for about 2 weeks, or until they are completely dry and crumbly. Once the flowers are dry, you can store them in an airtight container. To make the tea, boil 1 cup of water and add 1 tablespoon of dried calendula flowers. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve. You can also add honey or lemon to taste. Calendula tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum pain and inflammation. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to get the best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, is a popular remedy for gum pain. The tea is made from the leaves of the peppermint plant, which contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed, these compounds help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. The menthol in peppermint tea, in particular, has a numbing effect that can provide temporary relief from sharp pains and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint tea has antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent infection and promote healing in the gums. When used as a mouthwash or consumed as a tea, peppermint's soothing properties can help to calm irritated gums and provide comfort for people experiencing gum pain.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal tea for gum pain relief, start by gathering 1-2 teaspoons of dried Calendula flowers, which can be purchased from a health food store or grown at home. Next, boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for a minute. Add the dried Calendula flowers to the cooled water, and then let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the water. After the infusion, strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup. Discard the solids and let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before consumption. You can drink the Calendula tea 2-3 times a day, either on its own or mixed with other herbal teas, to help soothe and reduce gum pain. It's recommended to consult a healthcare professional before using Calendula tea, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies.

supplementary_best: To remedy gum pain, a combination of herbal teas can be effective. The best combination includes Zingiber officinale, Eucalyptus globulus, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. Eucalyptus globulus, or eucalyptus, has antibacterial properties that can help fight off infection and reduce inflammation. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe and calm irritated gums.  This combination is effective because it addresses the root causes of gum pain, including inflammation, infection, and irritation. By combining these three herbs, you can create a tea that targets multiple aspects of gum pain, providing relief and promoting healing. This combination is also gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for daily consumption. To make the tea, simply steep 1 teaspoon of each herb in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
supplementary_avoid: If you have gum pain, you may want to avoid certain herbal teas that can irritate the mouth or gums. One such herb is 'Cinchona officinalis', which contains quinine - a compound that can make gum pain worse. Another herb to avoid is 'Berberis vulgaris', whose roots and bark contain berberine, a compound that can dry out the mouth and irritate the gums. 'Hydrastis canadensis' is also not recommended, as it contains berberine and can cause mouth dryness and gum inflammation. Additionally, 'Artemisia absinthium' tea, which contains thujone, can irritate the mouth and worsen gum pain. Finally, 'Gelsemium sempervirens' tea, which contains gelsemine, can cause numbness and tingling in the mouth, making gum pain feel worse. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming any herbal teas, especially if you have gum pain or other health concerns.
supplementary_other_remedies: Gum pain can be caused by many things, including gum disease, tooth sensitivity, and mouth injuries. Besides herbal teas, there are other natural remedies that can help. For example, oil pulling is a technique where you swish oil in your mouth to remove bacteria and reduce inflammation. Coconut oil and sesame oil are popular choices because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help with gum pain. Another option is to use a saltwater rinse to reduce swelling and kill bacteria. Simply mix 1/2 teaspoon of salt with 8 ounces of warm water and swish it around your mouth for 30 seconds before spitting it out. Aloe vera gel can also be applied directly to the affected area to reduce swelling and pain. This is because aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe gum pain. Some people also find that applying a cold compress or an ice pack to the affected area can help numb the pain and reduce swelling. This is because the cold temperature can help constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation. Additionally, some studies suggest that probiotics can help prevent gum disease by promoting a healthy balance of bacteria in the mouth.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience gum pain often have other health issues related to inflammation and infection. For example, they may have mouth ulcers or canker sores, which can be painful and make eating and speaking difficult. These sores can be caused by a bacterial or viral infection, and medicinal herbs like aloe vera and echinacea can help soothe and heal them.  Gum pain is also often linked to sinus infections, as the sinus cavities and gums are connected through the bloodstream. When bacteria or viruses infect the sinuses, they can also cause gum inflammation and pain. Herbs like goldenseal and eucalyptus can help reduce sinus congestion and fight off infection.  Another common issue related to gum pain is digestive problems, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or acid reflux. The connection between gum health and digestive health is due to the presence of a bacteria called Porphyromonas gingivalis, which can cause both gum disease and digestive issues. Herbs like slippery elm and marshmallow root can help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation.  In addition, people with gum pain may also experience headaches and migraines, which can be caused by inflammation and tension in the face and jaw. Herbs like feverfew and willow bark can help reduce pain and inflammation, and also have natural anti-inflammatory properties.  Herbal remedies like clove oil and tea tree oil have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can help kill bacteria that cause gum infections and inflammation. They can be applied directly to the gums or used as a mouthwash to reduce pain and promote healing.
